desc: a method of filming where frames are shot much slower thantheir normal rate , allowing action to take place betweenframes , and giving the appearance of the action taking placemuch faster in the finished product ; often done for naturefilming ( the blooming of a flower , the movement of clouds ,etc. ) , allowing the viewer to witness the event compressedfrom real time ( hours or days ) into a few seconds ; ( one frameshot every 30 seconds over 24 hours of real time would equaltwo minutes of film time ) ; opposite of slow - motion

name: Time Lapse VCR (TL VCR)
desc: – A video recorder, most often in VHS format, that can prolong the video recording on a single tape up to 960 hours (this refers to a 180-minute tape). This type of VCR is often used in CCTV systems. The principle of operation is very simple – instead of having the video tape travel at a constant speed of 2.275 cm/s (which is the case with the domestic models of VHS VCRs), it moves with discrete steps that can be controlled. Time lapse VCRs have a number of other special func_x0002_tions very useful in CCTV, such as external alarm trigger, time and date superimposed on the video signal, alarm search and so on

name: Time Lapse Video Recording
desc: The intermittent recording of video signals at intervals to extend the recording time of the recording medium. It is usually measured in reference to a 3-hour (180-minute) tape.

name: Time Line
desc: This is the graphical interface used by most nonlinear editing software. You simply drag and drop your clips onto the time line then your transitions, effects, filters and titles.

name: Timeline (Menu)
desc: The time function that performs (runs) the keyframes of an effect in sequence and enables the timing of the effect to be modified.

name: Timeline (Titan)
desc: An Avolites Titan playback format that sequences multiple cues and playbacks to a piece of recorded music or a timecoded show. Provides frame-accurate synchronization to audio or external timecode.

name: Time Multiplex
desc: a) In the case of CCIR-601, a technique for transmitting three signals at the same time on a group of parallel wires (parallel cable).b) The technique of recording several cameras onto one time lapse VCR by sequentially sending camera pictures with a timed interval delay to match the time lapse mode selected on the recorder. See also Multiplex.

name: Time Offset Table (TOT)
desc: Optional DVB SI table that supplies the UTC time and data and shows the difference between UTC time and the local time for various geographical regions. The PID for this table is 0 x 0014,

name: Timer
desc: See Interval Timer.

name: Time Stamp
desc: a) A term that indicates the time of a specific action such as the arrival of a byte or the presentation of a presentation unit. b) A sampled value of a counter at an instant of time. It is used as a timing signal that may be contained in a synchronous data stream. c) An indication of a particular time instant relative to a time base.

name: Timing
desc: The process of selecting the printing values for color and density of successive scenes in a complete film to produce the desired visual effects.

name: Timing Jitter
desc: The variation in time of the significant instances (such as zero crossings) of a digital signal relative to a jitter-free clock above some low frequency (typically 10 Hz). It is preferable to use the original reference clock, but it is not usually available so a heavily averaged oscillator is used in the measurement.

name: Timing Model
desc: A model that specifies the semantic meaning of timing information, how it is incorporated (explicitly or implicitly) in the coded representation of information, and how it can be recovered at the terminal.

name: Timing Reference Mark
desc: This is the 50% point on the leading edge of the horizontal sync pulse. In an RGB system, the green signal’s horizontal sync pulse is used. In color difference formats, the Y signal’s horizontal sync pulse is used.

name: Timing Reference Signal Identification (TRS-ID)
desc: A four word reference used to maintain timing in serial composite digital systems.

name: Timing Reference Signals (TRS)
desc: A four word reference signal used in serial composite digital systems to synchronize the conversion of serial data back to parallel.

name: Tinnitus
desc: The Ringing In The Ears That Often Results From Prolonged Exposure To Very Loud Sound Levels. A Sound In The Ears, Such As Buzzing, Ringing, Or Whistling, Caused By Volume Knob Abuse!

name: tint
desc: the use of color to physically tint film stock to achieve adesired mood , usually done selectively by hand ; often usedby silent black - and - white films before the widespread use ofcolor film . See gel and sepia .

name: T Intervals
desc: See the definition of Sine-Squared Pulses.

name: Titan Mobile
desc: Avolites' compact PC wing that connects to a Windows PC running Titan software, providing hardware faders, encoders, and buttons for a portable but fully functional Titan console.

name: TitanNet
desc: Avolites' networking system for distributing additional DMX universes over Ethernet using Titan Network Processor (TNP) nodes. Allows a single Titan console to control up to 64 DMX universes.

name: Titan PC Suite
desc: The full Titan lighting control software from Avolites available for Windows PC, allowing complete Titan functionality on a standard computer with or without connected hardware.

name: Titan (software)
desc: Avolites' lighting control operating system running on all Avolites consoles (Diamond 9, Tiger Touch II, Sapphire Touch, Arena) and as Titan PC Suite on Windows. Known for its intuitive programming, shape generator, and live busking capabilities.

name: Title
desc: A caption or super is a graphic usually text from a character generator i.e., chyron, 3M or from a title camera (black/white high resolution camera).

name: Title Bar
desc: Located at the top of the application window, it contains the name of the application and sometimes the name of the open file.

name: Title Generator
desc: A black-and-white camera used for shooting titles that are electronically superimposed onto the video picture during shooting or editing. A more sophisticated device known as a character generator (CG) can generate titles directly.

name: Title Key
desc: A key effect which imposes a caption over a background scene. The source of the title key signal may be a character generator or a graphics camera.

name: Titler
desc: See Character Generator (CG).

name: title role
desc: the lead part in a movie or other production for an actor oractress , that is named after the title of the film

name: titles
desc: the words that appear on the film screen and conveyinformation ; categories of titles include : credit titles , maintitles , end titles , insert titles , and subtitles ; a creeper title , alsoknown as a roll - up title , refers to a film title that appears tomove solwly across the screen - vertically or horizontally ; insilent film , " titles " ( called title cards or intertitles ) included thewritten commentary and full screens of textual dialoguespliced within the action ; title design refers to the artisticmanner in which the title of a film is displayed on screen ;theworking title is the name by which a film is known while it isbeing made ( e.g. , during the filming of Psycho ( 1960 ) , it wasknown as Production 9401 ) ; see Movie Title Screens ; seealso credits

name: Titling
desc: The addition of text, symbols and graphic elements to a video image. Titles may be added to a video scene during shooting or in post- production. Sophisticated titling devices allow the user to prepare text and graphics in various sizes, fonts and colors to be triggered later, one-by- one, at appropriate places within a production. Many video cameras include basic titlers or permit externally-generated titles to be mixed with the video image during shooting. The Video TitleMaker 2000 is a powerful tool for titling.

name: TIVO (or REPLAY TV)
desc: Two brand names for a consumer video file serv- er. These units will continually record what you are watching on television, allowing you to immediately replay parts of the program, pause the pro- gram, or record for viewing later. It is expected that these units will eventu- ally be incorporated into Set-Top Boxes and are already available in some STBs used for Direct TV.

name: tix
desc: abbreviation for tickets

name: TLCI
desc: Television Lighting Consistency Index. A metric developed by the EBU for broadcast and camera applications measuring how accurately a light source renders color as captured by a camera, rather than by human perception.

name: TLS
desc: Transport Layer Security. A protocol providing a secure communications channel over the Internet, preventing eavesdropping, tampering, or message forgery.

name: Tm
desc: Abbreviation For Tour Manager. In Short, Manages Operations Needed For Successful Concert Touring. These Include, But Are Not Limited To, Travel Arrangements, Bill Pay, Event Advancing, Supervising Meet And Greets, Taking Point On Artist Responsibilities And General Problem Solving While On The Road.

name: TMC
desc: See TransMux Channel.

name: TMCC (Transmission and Multiplexing Configuration Control) TML
desc: See TransMux Layer.

name: TMC (Time Multiplex Component)
desc: An old CBS ATV proposal for delivery via two NTSC-capable DBS channels. One channel would carry a MAC signal of NTSC characteristics; the other would carry additional vertical resolution and widescreen panels. This was the first system to prove that widescreen seams could be rendered invisible.

name: TMDS
desc: Transition Minimized Differential Signaling. The physical layer signaling used by DVI and HDMI for high-speed data transmission. Uses differential pairs to minimize EMI. HDMI 2.1 uses FRL (Fixed Rate Link) instead.

name: TMS
desc: See TransMux Stream.

name: TNP
desc: Titan Network Processor. A rack-mountable Avolites node that processes additional DMX universes for a TitanNet system or can operate as a standalone automatic show player.

name: Toe
desc: On the characteristic curve for a photographic material (the plot of density vs. log exposure), that portion representing nonlinear response at the lower densities. For electronic image relationship to photographic negatives or positives.

name: Toggle
desc: Switch back and forth from one state or value to another (i.e., on, off, on, off, etc.) by alternately opening and closing an electric circuit.

name: Tolerance
desc: The allowable deviation from the stated nominal width or length.

name: Toll Quality
desc: Telephone voice quality.

name: Tonal, Adj.
desc: In Reference To Audible Sound, Capable Of Exciting An Auditory Sensation Having Pitch.

name: tone
desc: the mood or atmosphere of a film scene , often revealed bythe director in the way a film is directed , e.g. , serious ,humorous , satiric , amusing , etc.

name: Tone Burst
desc: A Short Signal Used In Acoustical Measurements To Make Possible Differentiating Desired Signals From Spurious Reflections.

name: Tony Award
desc: The Antoinette Perry Award for Excellence in Broadway Theatre. The highest honor in American theatre, presented annually by the Broadway League and the American Theatre Wing.

name: Tony nominations
desc: Annual nominations for the Antoinette Perry Awards (Tony Awards), announced several weeks before the ceremony, recognizing excellence in Broadway theatre productions.

name: Tool
desc: A graphic entity on the screen which is not an object.

name: Tools
desc: A tool is a technique that is accessible via the MPEG-4 system description language (MSDL) or described using the MSDL. Tools may, themselves, consist of combinations of tools. Examples are motion compensator, sub-band filter, audiovisual synchronizer, compositor.

name: toon
desc: abbreviation for cartoon

name: Top Field
desc: One of two fields that comprise a frame of interlaced video. Each line of a top field is spatially located immediately above the corre- sponding line of the bottom field.

name: Top Hat
desc: See The Definition For Snoot.

name: Top Layer
desc: The topmost layer (with the highest layer_id) of a scalable hierarchy.

name: topline
desc: to star ; or to be billed above the title of a film ; the topliner isthe star of a particular film

name: topper
desc: refers to the head of a company or organization

name: Torchiere
desc: An  Indirect Floor Lamp Sending All Or Nearly All Of Its Light Upward.

name: Tormentor
desc: Another Name For A Leg Curtain. These Are The First Masking Legs Located After The Main Curtain.

name: Toshiba
desc: One of the first television set manufacturers to demonstrate an IDTV set. Also a proponent of a widescreen ATV system utilizing high- frequency subcarriers to carry the side panels in a receiver-compatible, channel-compatible signal.

name: TOSLINK
desc: An optical fiber connection standard for digital audio developed by Toshiba. Common for audio output from CD/DVD players and game consoles.

name: To Source
desc: Video source that is supplying the video and/or audio that is being cut, dissolved or wipe to.

name: TOT
desc: See Time Offset Table.

name: Total Harmonic Distortion (Thd)
desc: Refers To A Device Adding Harmonics That Were Not In The Original Signal. For Example: A Device That Is Fed A 20Hz Sine Wave That Is Also Putting Out 40Hz, 80Hz, Etc. Not Usually A Factor In Most Modern Electronics, But Still A Significant Design Problem In Loudspeakers.

name: Total internal reflection
desc: The total reflection of light at a boundary between two optical media when the angle of incidence exceeds the critical angle.

name: Total Thickness
desc: Normally, the sum of the thicknesses of the base film and the magnetic coating. The total thickness governs the length of tape that can be wound on a given reel.

name: Touchpanel
desc: An LCD display with resistive or capacitive touch overlay used as a graphical user interface in a control system. Also called a touchscreen.

name: Touchscreen
desc: Term used for a special type of machine controller which has a matrix of photo voltaic transmitters and receivers across the face of a monitor such that by placing a finger on the desired point of the screen intersects this light matrix and automatically activates the corresponding switch.

name: tour de force
desc: literally " forceful turn " ( French ) ; usually refers to a lead actor'sperformance that was incredibly skillful , brilliant , notable ,masterful , reflecting a very high standard , and perfectlydisplaying the actor's ability ; compare to ' stealing a scene ' -the equivalent for a supporting actor role

name: Touring
desc: The process of taking a production to multiple venues. Touring productions require careful planning of get-in/get-out schedules, equipment transport (Bus & Truck), and adaptation of the show to different venue configurations.

name: TOV
desc: Threshold Of Visibility. The Impairment Level (Or D/U In Db) Beyond Which A Source Of Impairment Or Interference May Introduce Visible Deficiencies In More Sensitive Program Material. For All Tests, Tov Was Determined By Expert Observers.

name: TOV (Threshold of Visibility)
desc: A bit-error-rate HDTV threshold of 3 x 10-6, at which value the impairment effect first becomes visible in the picture.

name: TP
desc: Twisted Pair. Cable using small twisted wire pairs for network or telephone signals. Can be shielded (STP) or unshielded (UTP).

name: T-Pulse to Bar
desc: A term relating to frequency response of video equip- ment. A video signal containing equal amplitude T-pulse and bar portions is passed through the equipment and the relative amplitudes of the T-pulse and bar are measured at the output. A loss of response is indicated when one portion of the signal is lower in amplitude than the other.

name: Tracer
desc: See Current Tracer.

name: Track
desc: a) An area of tape surface that coincides with the location of the recorded magnetization produced by one record gap. b) A distinct element of audiovisual information, such as the picture, a sound track for a specific language, or the like. DVD-Video allows one track of video (with multiple angles), up to 8 tracks of audio, and up to 32 tracks of subpicture. c) One revolution of the continuous spiral channel of information recorded on a disc.

name: Track Buffer
desc: Circuitry (including memory) in a DVD player that provides a variable stream of data (up to 10.08 Mbps) to the system decoders of data coming from the disc at a constant rate of 11.08 Mbps (except for breaks when a different part of the disc is accessed).

name: Tracker
desc: A Device That Provides Numeric Coordinates To Identify The Current Position And/Or Orientation Of An Object Or User In Real Space.

name: Track Head
desc: An  Adjustable Luminaire That Connects To The Track In A Track Lighting System.

name: Tracking
desc: Is Any Shot Where The Camera Moves Alongside The Object(S) It Is Recording.

name: Tracking (lighting)
desc: A cue programming method where only changed parameter values are stored in each cue. Unchanged values 'track through' from previous cues. Standard behavior on most professional consoles including grandMA, Hog, and MagicQ.

name: Tracking Shot
desc: A shot containing camera movement.

name: tracking shot( or truck )
desc: a smooth shot in which the camera moves alongside( ' tracking within ' ) the subject , usually mounted on a dolly , in aside - to - side motion ( relative to the scene or the action ) ; alsoknown as following shot ; sometimes used interchangeably withdolly shot , pull back ( pull - out , push - out , widen - out orpush back ) shot , track back ( moving away ) or track in ( orpush - in )( moving forward ) , or zoom shot ; see alsoSteadicam

name: Track Lighting
desc: A Lighting System With An Electrically Fed Linear Track That Accepts One Or More Track Heads. The Track Heads Can Be Easily Relocated Along The Track.

name: Track Pitch
desc: The distance (in the radial direction) between the centers of two adjacent tracks on a disc. DVD-ROM standard track pitch is 0.74 mm.

name: Tracks
desc: The Rails On Which Curtains (Tabs) Run.

name: Track Shot
desc: Rotating Display Of The Same Scene. (See Also Dolly Shot And Pan Shot.)

name: Track Spacing
desc: The distance between the center lines of adjacent tracks.

name: Track Width
desc: The width of the track corresponding to a given record gap.

name: Traction Drive Winch
desc: Winch With A V-Grooved Drum That Uses Friction Between Cables And The Sides Of The Grooves To Engage The Pulling Cables.Increasing The Cable Tension Causes The Cables To Jam Tighter In The V-Grooves.

name: trademark
desc: refers to a personal touch or embellishment of an actor ,director , writer or producer within a film ; aka signature , callingcard .

name: trades
desc: refers to the professional magazines and publications thatreport the daily or weekly entertainment news of theentertainment industry .

name: trailer
desc: a short publicity film , preview , or advertisement composed ofshort excerpts and scenes from a forthcoming film or comingattraction , usually two - three minutes in length ; oftenpresented at the showing of another film . Historically , theseadvertisements were placed at the end of a newsreel orsupporting feature and so " trailed " them , hence the name ;also commonly known as preview ( s ) ; also , another name forthe tail - a length of blank leader ( strip of film ) at the end of areel ; a teaser is basically a very short trailer ( of 15-30seconds in length ) that only provides a few hints about thefilm ( a Web address , a few bars of music , a quick sequenceof images , specially - shot footage , etc. ) .

name: Trailing Edge
desc: The place on the record head where the recording actually takes place.

name: Training Signal
desc: A Philips-proposed signal to be used in a two-channel ATV transmission scheme that would alert the receiver to flaws that may have been introduced in the transmission of the second channel so that it can compensate for them.

name: Trajectory
desc: A curve using a set of control points to interpolate in- between points.

name: TRANS
desc: See Transition.

name: Transceiver
desc: A device that can operate as a transmitter, receiver, or both.

name: Transcode
desc: The Process Of Converting A File Or Program From One Format Or Resolution To Another.

name: Transcoder
desc: Device that converts one component format to another, e.g., to convert (Y, R-Y, B-Y) signals to (RGB) signals.

name: Transcoding
desc: a) Converting a data stream from one format to another, such as MPEG-1 to H.263, or an H.320 video conferencing session to

name: Transducer
desc: Device That Converts Acoustic Energy Into Electrical Energy (Microphone) Or Electrical To Acoustic (Loudspeaker).

name: Transfer Function
desc: The Output To Input Relationship Of A Structure. Mathematically It Is The Laplace Transform Of The Output Divided By The Laplace Transform Of The Input.

name: Transfer Function, Electro-Optic
desc: a) Display – The relationship between the video signal supplied to a display device and the luminance of the resulting image produced by that display device. b) Recorder, Film – The relationship between the video signal supplied to the recorder and the resultant illuminance exposing the film

name: Transfer Function, Monitor Electro-Optic
desc: The relationship between video input to the monitor and the luminance of the CRT. Monitors are required to conform to a narrower range of performance specifications than is expected of commercial receivers. Confirming these tighter tolerances requires attention to measurement details since, for example, the lumi- nance may vary if different areas of the tube face are selected. Light  output is routinely measured in the center of large, uniform “patches” or windows. Since there is significant “bleeding” of light within a CRT face,  the monitor transfer function also decreases with decreasing size of the windows (it is thus reduced for fine detail) and with increasing video level of the raster surrounding the windows.

name: Transfer Function, Opto-Electronic
desc: The relationship between scene luminances and the corresponding video signal. There may be several opto-electronic transfer functions for a single system, depending upon where in the progression of possible nonlinear processing, bandlimiting, etc., the video signal is being identified. When referred to the camera output before bandlimiting and processing, however, it is essentially a linear function.

name: Transfer Manager
desc: A tool that you access through the System Toolchest that you use to copy files to and from local and remote tapes or disks.

name: Transfer Rate
desc: The speed at which a certain volume of data is trans- ferred from a device such as a DVD-ROM drive to a host such as a person- al computer. Usually measured in bits per second or bytes per second. Sometimes confusingly used to refer to data rate, which is independent of the actual transfer system.

name: Transform
desc: The process or result of replacing a set of values with anoth- er set of values. A mapping of one information space onto another.

name: Transformation
desc: Refers to geometric motion or change to an object’s orientation (i.e., translate, rotate, scale).

name: Transform Coding
desc: a) A method of encoding a picture by dividing each picture into sub-pictures, performing a linear transformation on each sub- picture and then quantizing and coding the resulting coefficients. b) The conversion of a signal from one domain to another, e.g., the conversion of two-dimensional picture samples into the frequency domain by means of DCT, which is used in MPEG.

name: Transformer
desc: An Electronic Component Used To Step Audio Voltages Up Or Down To Send Signals Between Device Like Microphones And Mixing Consoles.

name: Transformer taps
desc: Transformers added to 8-ohm loudspeakers to adapt them for constant voltage distributed audio systems, with multiple output wattage options.

name: Transform, Systems
desc: Electronic production requires that images origi- nating in a multiplicity of systems and formats be made compatible in post-production for image processing. The necessary transforms may include temporal, spatial, resolution, colorimetry, etc.

name: Transient
desc: A Quick, High-Amplitude Burst Of Energy At The Beginning Of A Waveform,Often Associated With Percussive Instruments, Like A Drum Stick Striking A Snare Drum, Or Certain Consonants Of A Human Speech.

name: Transient Gain Distortions
desc: Transient gain distortion, also referred to as transient nonlinearity, is present when abrupt changes in APL temporarily affect signal amplitudes. The error is defined as the maximum transient departure in amplitude of sync pulse from the amplitude it had before the change to the amplitude after the change. Measurement of transient gain distortions is done as an out of service test and should be checked for transitions of low to high APL and high to low APL. If the transient gain distortion only affects the sync pulse and then not so severely as to cause the sync to be unusable, then the viewable picture would not be affected. However if the sync pulse is affected then the rest of the picture is also normally affected and when transient gain distortions affect the picture, it appears as abnormal transient brightness effects. A test signal generator capable of producing a “bouncing” flat field signal is used to test for transient gain distortions. A typical signal is shown below. The time between bounces (APL level changes) must be longer then the transient effects so that all the transient effects can be viewed before the next APL change occurs.

name: Transient Nonlinearity
desc: See the discussion on transient gain distortion.

name: Transient Response
desc: The Ability Of A Component To Respond Quickly And Accurately To Transients. Transient Response Affects Reproduction Of The Attack And Decay Characteristics Of A Sound.

name: Transients
desc: Signals which endure for a brief time. These may include overshoots, damped sinusoidal waves, etc., and, therefore, additional quali- fying information is necessary.

name: Transistor
desc: An Electronic Component Used As Either An Amplifier, Taking A Very Small Input Current And Producing A Larger Output Current, Or A Switch.

name: Transition
desc: a) A change from one picture to another. Any mix, wipe, cut, non-additive mix, or introduction of a key. b) The moving of a fader arm  or initiating an “auto transition” to accomplish any of the above effects.

name: Transition Effect
desc: An effect (e.g., barn doors, wipe) where the elements of one clip blend with another during transition.

name: Transition Mode
desc: Exclusively on the AVC series, an operator may choose automatic transitions that are not linear, that is that they do not have the same rate as they progress. One may choose logarithmic, starting rapidly and finishing slowly; exponential, starting slowly and finishing rapidly; or sinusoidal, starting and finishing slowly but fast in the middle.

name: transition( or transitionaltechnique ordevice )
desc: one of several ways of moving from one shot or scene to thenext , including such transitional effects or shots asa cut , fade , dissolve , and wipe ; a transition focus between twoscenes means the current scene goes out of focus and thenext scene comes into focus

name: Transition Rate
desc: The duration of an automatic transition from one bus to the other is defined as the transition rate. The transition rate may be applied to a mix, wipe or E key, and is operator selectable from 0 to 9.9 seconds.

name: Translate
desc: To move an object without rotation, in a straight line, either left or right, up or down, in or out, or any combination thereof, in three- dimensional space.

name: Translating
desc: The process for converting one color difference signal format to another. See the discussion on Matrix.

name: Translational Extrusion
desc: In translational extrusion, the silhouette follows a linear path.

name: Translator
desc: a) Broadcast station that rebroadcasts signals of other sta- tions without originating its own programming. b) A device used to convert one component set to another, e.g., to convert Y, R-Y, B-Y signals to RGB signals.

name: Transliminal Set
desc: A Physical Set That Extends Beyond The Boundaries Of Led Volume Doors To Mitigate Theproscenium Feeling , Which Can Occur When Set Builds All Feel The Same Size In Relation To Thevolume .

name: Transmission
desc: The Ability Of Light To Penetrate Through Something.

name: Transmission Aperture
desc: A number used to compare the amounts of light passed through optical systems, such as camera lenses. Transmission aperture takes into consideration both the F-stop (geometric aperture) and the amount of light absorbed or reflected in the optical system.

name: Transmission Factor
desc: The Ability Of A Medium To Allow For The Transmission Of Light, Expressed As A Percentage.

name: Transmission Standard
desc: A standard to be used for transmitting signals to the home, not necessarily for producing them. The scanning structure of NTSC is identical for both production and transmission, but this need not be the case in ATV schemes. For example, an HDEP standard of 1125 scanning lines might be used with a transmission standard of 1050 lines. Standards converters translate one standard into another.

name: Transmitter
desc: An Electronic Device Which Produces Radio Frequency (Rf) Signal. The Transmitter Itself Generates A Radio Frequency Alternating Current, Which Is Applied To The Antenna. When Excited By This Alternating Current, The Antenna Radiates Radio Waves.

name: Transmit (Tx)
desc: In fiber optics, the light source (LED or laser) at the transmitting end.

name: TransMux
desc: A generic abstraction for delivery mechanisms (computer networks, etc.) able to store or transmit a number of multiplexed elemen- tary streams or FlexMux streams. This specification does not specify a TransMux Layer.

name: TransMux Channel (TMC)
desc: A logical channel that carries data from one FlexMux stream packetized in a sequence of PL-PDUs.

name: TransMux Entity
desc: An instance of the MPEG-4 systems resource that processes TransMux-PDUs associated to one TransMux stream. This is what may be loosely called the TransMux (de)multiplexer.

name: TransMux Layer (TML)
desc: A logical MPEG-4 Systems Layer between the FlexMux Layer and the lower network layers used to interleave one or more FlexMux streams, packetized in PL-PDUs, into one TransMux stream. The TransMux layer may be specified outside MPEG-4, e.g., ATM, H.223, TCP, UDP, MPEG-2 TS, etc.

name: TransMux Protocol Data Unit Header (TransMux-PDU Header)
desc: Information preceding the TransMux-PDU payload. It usually specifies the TransMux channel(s) to which the payload of this TransMux-PDU belongs. It may carry further information, depending on the selected TransMux Layer.

name: TransMux Protocol Data Unit Payload (TransMux-PDU Payload)
desc: The data field of a TransMux-PDU.

name: TransMux Protocol Data Unit (TransMux-PDU)
desc: The smallest protocol unit of a TransMux stream exchanged between peer TransMux entities. It consists of TransMux-PDU header and TransMux-PDU payload. It carries data from one or more TransMux channel.

name: TransMux Stream (TMS)
desc: A sequence of TransMux-PDUs originating from one or more TransMux channels flowing through one network connection.

name: TransMux User
desc: An MPEG-4 systems entity that makes use of the serv- ices of the TransMux Layer, typically a Protection Layer entity.

name: Transparency
desc: Listening Term. An Analog That Can Be Best "Pictured" In Photography. The More "Transparent" The Sound, The Clearer The Auditory Picture.

name: Transparency Frame
desc: In the transparency technique first-frame analy- sis, the first frame of the video file. It contains no video data, but merely supplies the color or range of colors to be rendered as transparent. See First-Frame Analysis, Transparency.

name: Transponder
desc: Satellite transmitter/receiver that picks up signals trans- mitted from earth, translates them into new frequencies and amplifies them before retransmitting them back to ground.

name: Transport
desc: Term used for any machine using motors usually meaning a VTR, DTR or video disk.

name: Transportation
desc: The delivery in physical form of a program prepared for distribution. The completed program may be in the form of a tape recording, a film print, an optical disc, etc.

name: Transport Stream
desc: A multiplex of one or more programs that are carried in packets. Demultiplexing is achieved by different packet IDs (PIDs). See PSI, PAT, PMT, and PCR.

name: transport_stream_id
desc: A unique identifier of a TS within an original network.

name: Transport Stream Packet Header
desc: The leading fields in a transport stream packet up to and including the continuity_counter field.

name: Transverse
desc: Across the width of the tape.

name: Trap
desc: A Trapdoor Set In The Stage Floor.

name: Trap door
desc: An opening through the stage floor used for dramatic entrances, exits, and special effects. Also simply called a trap.

name: Trapezoidal Error
desc: A change in the angle of a recorded helical scan track. Can result in mistracking.

name: Traps
desc: Sections of the stage floor that are removable, allowing access to the space below the stage. Can be replaced with trapdoors, elevators, or stairs, or left as an open pit for special effects.

name: trash film
desc: refers to second - run , low - budget films that are deliberatelyover - the - top , infantile , amateurish , sometimes excessivelygory or raunchy which are intended to shock , disgust , andrepel mainstream audiences , and appeal to non - traditionalaudiences . Sometimes described as a sub - categoryofexploitation and cult films , or called a ' turkey ' film . Comparewith sexploitation , B - films , and Z - films .

name: Travel
desc: The Path Of Moving Stage Equipment And The Distance Moved.

name: Traveler
desc: A Curtain On A Track That Can Be Opened Or Closed To Reveal Or Mask A Portion Of The Stage.

name: Traveling Matte
desc: A process shot in which foreground action is superim- posed on a separately photographed background by optical printing.

name: Traveller
desc: A curtain or cloth that travels horizontally across the stage opening on a track. Used as a scene divider or to make changes behind.

name: travelogue
desc: a film made for the purpose of showing scenes from foreign ,exotic places

name: Traverse stage
desc: A stage configuration where the audience is seated on two opposite sides of a long, narrow performance area.

name: Tread Diameter
desc: The Diameter Of A Sheave Measured At The Bottom Of Its Groove.

name: Tread Pressure
desc: This Indicator Of The Radial Bearing Pressure (P) Of A Rope Against A Sheave Groove Is Taken As The Tension (T) In The Rope Divided By The Tread Radius (R) Of The Sheave Times The Rope Diameter (D). P=2T/Dd

name: treatment
desc: a detailed literary summary or presentation of a film's story ,with action and characters described in prose form , oftenused to market and / or sell a film project or script ; a completedtreatment is a late stage in the development of a screenplayafter several story conferences have incorporated changesinto the script ; contrast to a synopsis ( a brief summation of afilm ) ;

name: Treble
desc: The Higher Frequencies Of The Audible Spectrum.

name: Trellis Coding
desc: Trellis coding is a source coding technique that has resulted in numerous publications and some very effective source codes. Unfortunately, the computational burden of these codes is tremendous and grows exponentially with the encoding rate. A trellis is a transition diagram, that takes time into account, for a finite state machine. Populating a trellis means specifying output symbols for each branch, specifying an initial  state yields a set of allowable output sequences. A trellis coder is defined as follows: given a trellis populated with symbols from an output alphabet and an input sequence x of length n, a trellis coder outputs the sequence of bits corresponding to the output sequence x that maximizes the SNR of the encoding.

name: Trellis Diagram
desc: The time sequence of the bits (DVB-S) is predefined by convolutional coding and, like the state diagram of a finite automaton, is represented as a trellis diagram.

name: Triad
desc: Three colored phosphor dots on the faceplate of a tri-color CRT. Some tri-color CRTs use vertical stripes of different color phosphors or vertically oriented oblong dots. These dots or stripes are the ultimate deter- minants of maximum horizontal resolution. When the dots are round, they are also the maximum determinants of vertical resolution. The finer the dot pitch, the higher the resolution, since it is not possible to reduce the size  of a black-and-white pixel below the size of one triad. Triad spacing also cannot be optimized for all numbers of scanning lines. Thus, a tube opti- mized for 1125 scanning lines will not yield optimum performance with a signal of 1050 scanning lines, or vice versa. Neither black-and-white CRTs nor the three single-color CRTs used in most projection TV sets suffer from these limitations as their faceplates are uniformly covered with a layer of phosphor and resolution is ultimately determined by the size of the electron beam and the projection optics. Picture tubes with striped apertures can deal effectively with multiple scanning rates, but still restrict horizontal resolution to the width of three stripes.

name: Trichromatic
desc: The technical name for RGB representation of color to create all the colors in the spectrum.

name: Trick Modes
desc: A generic analog term that has carried over to digital media functions such as fast forward, rewind, stop, pause.

name: Trigger
desc: Slang term for the button on the video camera or camcorder that when depressed, sends a signal to the videotape recorder to begin or stop recording.

name: Trigger Arbor Release
desc: This Is An Auxiliary System That Is Added To A Fire Safety Curtain Rig So The Curtain Can Be In Balance For Normal Operation But Also Allow Automatic Closure In An Emergency By Adding Weight To The Curtain. It Basically Consists Of A Guided Auxiliary Weight Arbor, A Catch Arbor That Is Tied To The Curtain And Receives The Auxiliary Weight Arbor (Trigger Arbor),  And A Triggering Device That Releases  The Trigger Arbor In Response To A Loss  Of Tension In The Fire Line.

name: Tri-level Sync
desc: This synchronization signal is sued within high definition analog formats and is a three-level pulse which goes from 0 to -300 mV and then rises to +300 mV before returning to 0 mV. The reference point OH is defined as the 50% point of the positive rising edge of the signal and is at the 0 mV level. This simplifies sync separator design and increases sync stability.

name: trilogy
desc: a group of three films that together compose a larger narrativeand are related in subject or theme

name: Trim
desc: In Audio Mixers, The Gain Adjustment For The First Amplification Stage Of The Mixer. The Trim Control Allows The Mixer To Accommodate The Wide Range Of Input Signal Levels That Come From Real-World Sources. It Is Important To Set The Trim Control Correctly; Its Setting Determines The Overall Noise Performance In That Channel Of The Mixer. See Mic Preamp.

name: Trim Chain
desc: A Length Of Chain Placed Between A Lift Line And A Pipe Batten Or Scenic Element To Connect Them And To Facilitate Minor Height Adjustment Of The Load.

name: Trim Curves
desc: Curves that define holes on or parts cut away from a surface; they are linked to the surface.

name: Trim (fly)
desc: The height at which a batten or other flown object hangs. Setting the correct trim is the process of adjusting fly lines to achieve the desired position.

name: Trimming
desc: Editing a clip on a frame-by-frame basis, or editing clips in relationship to one another.

name: Trim (rigging)
desc: US term for the pre-plotted height of a flown scenic piece or lighting bar. Equivalent to a Dead in the UK. Set and marked on the fly rope for repeatable positioning.

name: Trim Ring
desc: A Plastic Or Metal Ring Used To Cover And Seal The Edges Of Holes That Are Cut Into Ceilings To Install Recessed  Luminaires.

name: Triphosphor
desc: See Rare-Earth Phosphors.

name: Triple Header
desc: A Tee Bar With Three Individual, Or Sets Of, Studs Or Receivers.

name: triple threat
desc: refers to an actor or actress who can sing , dance and actskillfully and equally well on a consistent basis ; usuallyapplicable to performers in the musicals genre ; it also couldrefer to a person who can act , direct , and screenwrite !

name: Tripod
desc: A three-legged video camera or camcorder mounting device that provides steady, tireless service.

name: Tripod Dolly
desc: A combination tripod and dolly.

name: Tripod head
desc: The top part of a tripod that mounts the camera and enables smooth pan and tilt movements.

name: Tripped
desc: A Curtain Or Scenic Element Is Lifted By A Second Set Of Lines Attached At The Bottom Or Intermediate Point On The Piece. Pulling The Lines Will Cause The Piece To Fold In Half Or Thirds.Note: If The Piece Is Counterbalanced,The Weight Balance Will Shift As The Piece Is Tripped.

name: Tri-Scan
desc: Term for the technique of sub-sampling each NTSC pixel into three sub-pixels used in the HD-NTSC ATV scheme.

name: Tristimulus
desc: A three-valued signal that can match nearly all colors of visible light in human vision. This is possible because of the three types of photoreceptors in the eye. RGB, YCbCr, and similar signals are tristimulus, and can be interchanged by using mathematical transformations (subject to possible loss of information).

name: Tristimulus Colorimeter
desc: A color measurement device that measures the three primary color components (red, green, blue) of a light source to calculate and display color values.

name: Tristimulus Values
desc: a) Amounts of the three reference color stimuli, in a given trichromatic system, required to match the color of the stimulus considered. Note: In the CIE standard colorimetric systems, the tristimulus values are represented by the symbols X, Y, Z and X~, Yn, Zn. b) The amounts of the three reference or matching stimuli required to give a match with the light considered in a given trichromatic system.

name: Troffer
desc: A  Recessed Luminaire That Is Installed In The Plenum With The Opening Flush With The Ceiling. Typically Rectangular Or Square In Shape, As In A 2-Foot By 4-Foot Luminaire.

name: Troubleshoot
desc: To seek the cause of a malfunction or erroneous program behavior in order to remove the malfunction.

name: Troubleshooting Tree
desc: Flow diagram consisting of tests and measure- ments used to diagnose and locate faults in a product.

name: Trs
desc: Acronym For Tip-Ring-Sleeve, The Three Parts Of A Two-Conductor (Plus Shield) Phone Plug. Since The Plug Or Jack Can Carry Two Signals And A Common Ground, Trs Connectors Are Often Referred To As Stereo Or Balanced Plugs Or Jacks. Another Common Trs Application Is For Insert Jacks, Used For Inserting An External Processor Into The Signal Path.

name: TRS-ID
desc: See Timing Reference Signal Identification.

name: TRT (Total Running Time)
desc: Usually expressed in hr:min:sec:frames or min:sec:frames.

name: Truck
desc: Term used for a type of camera movement where the camera actually moves left to right (vice versa) across a scene.

name: Trucking (Crabbing)
desc: A Tracking Shot That Moves From Side To Side Instead Of Toward Or Away From The Subject.

name: Truck (stage)
desc: A rolling platform on castors used to move large scenic pieces on and off stage quickly. Also called a wagon.

name: True Color
desc: An image in which each pixel is individually represented using three color components, such as RGB or Y’CbCr. The color compo- nents of each pixel may be independently modified.

name: True NTSC
desc: A concept of an idealized NTSC that is identical throughout the NTSC world. Unfortunately, the NTSC standards are loose enough to allow various sub-channel schemes, though these schemes may be mutually incompatible. It is possible that some years from now an NTSC television set designed for one form of enhanced NTSC may be receiver- incompatible with transmission of another form of enhanced NTSC.

name: Truespeech
desc: Truespeech is a codec used for low bandwidth encoding of speech (not music). It was created by the DSP Group. It is available on Microsoft Windows 98 among other systems.

name: Truncation
desc: a) Deletion of lower significant bits on a digital system. Usually results in digital noise. b) Shortening the word length of a sample or coefficient by removing low-order bits. c) To terminate a computational process in accordance with some rule. For example, when digital mixing or other operations create extra bits per sample (such as 16 bits from multi- plication of two 8-bit samples), it is usually necessary at some point to truncate (or round) the result back to the original bit length, and to apply some rule to the correction of the part retained. Various rules have been introduced for how this may be done with digital video images for the least noticeable result.

name: Truss
desc: A Structure Fabricated From Various Truss Sections, Used To Hold Up Luminaires, Scenery, And/Or Other Production Equipment. Truss Can Be Ground-Supported, Or Suspended Overhead With Rigging.

name: Truss Batten
desc: Two Or More Pipes Or Other Linear Members Fabricated Together With Cross Bracing In A Trussed Configuration. Used In Place Of A Pipe Batten For Heavy Loads Or Extended Distances Between Lift Lines.

name: Truss Section
desc: A Sturdy, Lightweight Support Structure Fabricated From Tubular Aluminum In Various Shapes And Sizes.

name: Truss Spot
desc: A Short Throw Follow Spot Operated From A Truss Rig.

name: Ts
desc: Acronym For Tip-Sleeve, The Two Parts Of A Sin_x0002_Gle Conductor (Plus Shield) Phone Plug. Ts Con_x0002_Nectors Are Sometimes Called Mono Or Unbalanced Plugs Or Jacks. A 1/4” Ts Phone Plug Or Jack Is Also Called A Standard Phone Plug Or Jack.

name: TSB (Telecommunication Standardization Bureau)
desc: The executive arm of the Telecommunication Standardization Sector, and is headed by an elected Director. The Director of TSB is responsible for annually updating the work programme of the Sector approved by world telecommu- nication standardization assemblies, in consultation with the chairpersons of the ITU-T study groups and the Telecommunication Standardization Advisory Group.

name: TSDT (Transport Stream Description Table)
desc: A PSI table defined in MPEG-2 systems. The TSDT is composed of a section header followed by a descriptor loop (and in constrained to carry only descriptors as a payload in the descriptor loop). As stated in MPEG-2 systems, the descriptors carried in this table are scoped to the entire transport stream.

name: TS Header
desc: The first four bytes of each TS packet contain the data (PID) required for the demultiplexer in addition to the sync byte (0 x 47). These bytes are not encoded.

name: TSN
desc: Time-Sensitive Networking. An IEEE 802.1 set of standards extending Ethernet with deterministic, time-synchronized delivery for AV and industrial applications. Foundation for AVB and Milan audio networking.

name: T-STD (Transport Stream System Target Decoder)
desc: A decoder having a certain amount of buffer memory assumed to be present by an encoder.

name: T Steps
desc: See the definition of Sine-Squared Pulses.

name: TTL
desc: 1) Transistor-to-Transistor Logic. A digital signal of 4–5V peak-to-peak with 6–10 ft distance limitation. 2) Time to Live — the number of router hops permissible for a multicast packet.

name: TTL (Thru-the-Lens)
desc: Viewing or color measuring.

name: TTL (Transistor-Transistor Logic)
desc: Family of digital integrated circuits that have bipolar transistor inputs and outputs. b) Term used in digital electronics mainly to describe the ability of a device or circuit to be con- nected directly to the input or output of digital equipment. Such compatibil- ity eliminates the need for interfacing circuitry. TTL signals are usually limited to two states, low and high, and are thus much more limited than analog signals.

name: TTS (Text-to-Speech)
desc: Describes a software program that prompts a computer-generated voice to recite the words in a computer file.

name: TTY (Teletypewriter)
desc: A telecommunication device that enables conver- sation in printed form over the telephone.

name: Tube
desc: A Glass Device Free Of Air, Thus Creating A Vacuum, That Amplifies An Audio Signal In Various Kinds Of Circuits. Commonly Seen In Guitar Amplifiers And Preamplifiers.

name: tubthump
desc: a term that denotes to promote or draw attention to ; usuallyconducted by publicists , advertisers , and agents ; from theancient show business custom of actors wandering thestreets banging on tubs and drums to draw an audiencetogether

name: Tunable Laser
desc: A laser in which the central wavelength can be varied or optimized for a particular application.

name: Tunable white
desc: An LED lighting system capable of smoothly adjusting color temperature across a range (typically 2700K–6500K) to match time-of-day, occupant preference, or task requirements. Used in human-centric lighting.

name: Tune A Pa
desc: To Electronically Optimize A Speaker System, Depending On The Physical Environment Of The Venue. This Includes Both Room Size And Acoustics, As Well As The Atmospheric Conditions At Any Given Time.

name: Tuner
desc: An element of a television set that allows the user to select specific signals and frequencies (channels) to be shown on the picture tube and played through the speaker.

name: Tungsten-Halogen Lamp
desc: See Halogen Incandescent  Lamp.

name: Tuning Frequency
desc: The Helmholtz Resonant Frequency Of A Box. Also Refers To The Resonant Frequency Of Other Types Of Systems.

name: turnaround
desc: refers to a film or project that has been abandoned by astudio and is no longer active ( and now available for beingshopped to another studio )

name: Turn Down, Please
desc: What Audio Engineers Say To Musicians Who Have Excessive Stage Volume.

name: TV Crossover Links
desc: A Type Of Enhancement Which Notifies Users That There Is Enhanced Or Web Content Associated With A Program Or An Advertisement. A Tv Crossover Link Appears As A Small Icon In The Corner Of The Tv Screen At A Point In Time Determined By Content Producers. Clicking The Link Displays A Panel, Giving The Viewer An Option To Go To The Content Enhancement (Web Site) Or Continue Watching Tv. If The Viewer Chooses To Go To The Web Site, The Receiver Connects To The Site, While The Current Program Or Advertisement Remains On-Screen. Pressing The View Button On The Remote Control Or Keyboard Returns To Tv Viewing. The Term Is A Trademark Of The Microsoft Corporation.

name: TVCT
desc: See Terrestrial Virtual Channel Table.

name: TVL
desc: TV line. A resolution specifying alternating black-and-white lines displayable horizontally or vertically. Different from pixel count on fixed pixel devices.

name: TV Lines
desc: Measure of resolution. A TV line is either black or white, so  two TV lines (one black and one white) form one cycle of spatial resolution. TV lines are often confused with scanning lines. For vertical resolution, scanning lines multiplied by the Kell factor (and, when appropriate, by the interlace coefficient) yield TV lines.

name: TWAIN
desc: A scan control program that pops up within an application to allow for the adjustment of brightness, contrast, etc.

name: Tweak
desc: To Make Small Edits Or Changes To An Audio Signal Or Mix.

name: Tweeker
desc: A small screwdriver for making sensitive adjustments to audio/visual and other electronic equipment.

name: Tweening
desc: The feature that fills in the frames between two images so the movement appears smoother.  See also Keyframing.

name: Tweeter
desc: A Small Loudspeaker Designed For The Reproduction Of High- Frequency Sounds.

name: TWG (Technical Working Group)
desc: A general term for an industry working group.

name: Twinkle
desc: A sparkling effect that can be caused by sub-sampling, since the finest detail is transmitted at a rate below the flicker frequency (and sometimes even below the fusion frequency).

name: Twin-Tube Lamp
desc: A Single-Ended Fluorescent Lamp With The Tube Bent Into A Very Tight “U”  Or “H”   Shape.

name: Twisted-Pair
desc: A cable composed of two small insulated conductors twisted together. Since both wires have nearly equal exposure to any interference, the differential noise is slight.

name: twist ending
desc: a film that is marketed as having a surprise ending thatshouldn't be revealed ( as a spoiler ) to those who haven't seenthe picture

name: Twist-Lock Connector
desc: A Commonly Used Type Of Locking Blade Connector That Requires A Twisting Action To Lock The Mating Connectors Together, Manufactured By Harvey Hubbel, Inc. The Name "Twist-Lock" Is Trademarked.

name: Twitter
desc: A flickering of fine horizontal edges caused by interlaced scan- ning. A fine line appearing in only one field is presented below the flicker frequency; therefore, it flickers. Twitter is eliminated in line doubling schemes that change from interlaced to progressive scanning, as most of the IDTV schemes do. Interestingly, twitter was much less of a problem in the early days of NTSC, than it is today, because early cameras and dis- plays didn’t have sufficient detail to confine an edge to one scanning line.

name: two - fer
desc: slang for coupons that discount an film's admission price to" two for " the price of one

name: two - hander
desc: refers to a film with only two characters

name: two - reeler
desc: in the silent era , this referred to a film lasting a little over 20minutes

name: Two’s Complement Numbers
desc: a) Numbering system commonly used to represent both positive and negative numbers. The positive numbers in two’s complement representation are  identical to the positive numbers in standard binary. However, the Two’s complement representation of a negative number is the complement of the absolute binary value plus 1. Note that the eighth or most significant bit indicates the sign: 0 = plus, 1 = minus. b) The number calculated so that each bit of a binary number is inverted (ones are replaced with zeros and vice versa), then one (=000...0001b) is added ignoring the overflow.

name: two - shot
desc: a medium or close - up camera shot of two people ( often indialogue with each other ) , framed from the chest up ; oftenused to provide a contrast between the two characters ;compare to three - shot

name: Two-Track Recording
desc: On 1/4” wide tape, the arrangement by which only two channels of sound may be recorded, either as a stereo pair in one direction or as separate monophonic tracks (usually in opposite directions).

name: Two Wire Interconnect
desc: Interconnect consisting of two wires. One wire transports the luminance signal while the other transports the multi- plexed chrominance signals. This system allows efficient dubbing between recorders because recorders normally record the luminance on one tape channel and the two color difference signal on a single channel. To record the two color difference signals on a single channel, the two color difference signals are compressed and then multiplexed together..Transferring the two video signals between tape recorders in the two wire format prevents the two tape recorders from having to do additional signal processing.

name: Type C
desc: SMPTE standard for 1-inch non-segmented helical video recording format.

name: typecasting
desc: when an actor or actress is commonly ( but unfairly ) identified ,associated with , or ' stereotyped ' by a particular characterrole ; casting against type is the reverse oftypecasting ; typagerefers to director Eisenstein's theory ofcasting that shunned professional actors in favor of ' types ' orrepresentative characters

name: U
desc: The B-Y signal after a weighting factor of 0.493 has been applied. The weighting is necessary to reduce peak modulation in the composite signal.

name: UAD
desc: Universal Audio DSP. Universal Audio's proprietary hardware-accelerated DSP platform for running high-quality analog hardware emulations. UA plug-ins are available on some consoles (DiGiCo, SSL Live) via SD-Racks and server integration.

name: UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver Transmitter)
desc: a) A serial to parallel and parallel to serial converter. b) A serial interface which serial- izes parallel data and inserts start, stop, and parity bits. It may also change a serial data stream into parallel bits or bytes and separate start, stop, and parity bits.

name: UC
desc: Unified Communications. A concept describing a consistent unified user interface across multiple devices and media types including messaging, voice, web/video conferencing, and data sharing.

name: UCaaS
desc: Unified Communications as a Service. Cloud-based delivery of voice, video, messaging, and collaboration tools. Providers include Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Cisco Webex, and RingCentral.

name: UCI
desc: User Control Interface. In Q-SYS, a customizable touchpanel or web-based interface designed in Q-SYS Designer providing end-users with intuitive control of the system.

name: U-Clamp
desc: A "U" Shaped Clamp That Attaches Onto A Pipe And Locks With The Aid Of A Bolt, That When Tightened, Bites Into The Pipe And Locks The Clamp In Place. It Also Has A Secondary Bolt For The Attachment Of Equipment Of Light-To- Moderate Weight.

name: UDF Bridge
desc: a) A “bridge” ties several specifications together, In DVD, bridges are drawn to UDF, MPEG-2 and Dolby C-3. b) A combination of UDF and ISO 9660 file system formats that provides backward-compatibili- ty with ISO 9660 readers while allowing full use of the UDF standard.

name: UDF (Universal Disc Format)
desc: A standard developed by the Optical Storage Technology Association designed to create a practical and usable subset of the ISO/IEC 13346 recordable, random-access file system and volume structure format.

name: UDP
desc: User Datagram Protocol. Connectionless protocol with no guarantee of delivery, ordering, or duplicate protection. Low overhead makes it preferred for AVoIP multicast streams where low latency is critical and some packet loss is acceptable.

name: UDP (User Datagram Protocol)
desc: A transport protocol in the Internet suite of protocols. UDP, like TCP, uses IP for delivery; however, unlike TCP, UDP provides for exchange of datagrams without acknowledgements or guaranteed delivery.

name: UDTV (Ultra Definition TV)
desc: UDTV with a 2,000-line (or more) display is being contemplated in Japan. The ideas underline the importance of scalability in future broadcast technology, and suggest that rigid standards will only have a limited life span. The MPEG-2 syntax would support the level of resolution found in UDTV, but actual tests of conformance at this resolution are not planned so far. In addition, a question of interoperability with other digital TV services will also have to be investigated.

name: UHD
desc: Ultra High Definition. Resolutions above Full HD: 4K UHD (3840×2160), 8K UHD (7680×4320). Defined by ITU-R BT.2020. Offers four times the pixel count of HD at each step.

name: UHF
desc: Ultra High Frequency. Television broadcast frequencies between 300 and 3000 MHz on channels 14–83.

name: UHP
desc: Ultra High Performance. A mercury arc lamp technology by Philips (1995) for commercial and consumer projectors.

name: UI (Unit Interval)
desc: – In information technology, the user interface (UI) is everything designed into an information device with which a human being may interact – including display screen, keyboard, mouse, light pen, the appearance of a desktop, illuminated characters, help messages, and how an application program or a Web site invites interaction and responds to it. In early computers, there was very little user interface except for a few buttons at an operator’s console. The user interface was largely in the form of punched card input and report output

name: Ul
desc: Abbreviation For Underwriters' Laboratories, Inc.

name: Ultimate Tensile Strength
desc: The force per unit cross-sectional area required to break a tape or length of base film, usually given in pounds per square inch (psi). Ultimate tensile strength is also quoted in terms of pounds per tape sample of given width and base thickness.

name: Ultimatte
desc: Trade name of a high-quality special effects system similar in application to a chroma key switcher. Electronic implementation of the "blue screen" used for motion picture special effects.

name: Ultra High Frequency (UHF)
desc: The frequency band (300 MHz-3,000 MHz). In television, UHF refers to a subset of that band, the range from 470 MHz to 890 MHz, once allocated to TV channels 14 through 83. Demands of other transmission services (such as police radios) have eaten into both the lower and the upper reaches of the UHF TV allocations. Taboos eliminate still more channels. Nevertheless, the UHF TV band is seen by many ATV proponents as the most likely place to situate receiver- incompatible and augmentation ATV channels.

name: Ultra Physical Contact (UPC)
desc: A fiber optic endface polish achieving typically -60 dB return loss in singlemode applications. The most common polish for digital fiber optic connectors. Not compatible with APC connectors.

name: Ultra SCSI (Ultra Wide SCSI)
desc: Currently, the newest and best kind of drives for DV. New technology makes these drives better than AV optimized.

name: Ultrasonic (Type Of Motion Detector)
desc: Ultrasonic Motion Detectors Emit High-Frequency Sound Waves (Too High For The Human Ear To Hear), Which Are Reflected By Objects And Room Surfaces To A Receiver Located In The Detector. The Reflected Waves Set Up A Static Wave Pattern; Any Disturbance In This Pattern Alters The Frequency  Of The Reflected Wave, Which Is Detected By The Receiver. The Receiver Then Activates Whatever Device The Detector Controls (Usually An Alarm System Or One Or More Luminaires). See Also Motion Detector.

name: Ultra Violet (Uv)
desc: Radiation That Is Shorter In Wavelength And Higher In Frequency Than Visible Violet Light.

name: Ultraviolet (Uv) Radiation
desc: Any Radiant Energy Within The Wavelength Range Of 10 To 380 Nanometers.

name: U - matic
desc: refers to 3/4 inch magnetic tape , originally a professionalcassette tape format now being supplanted by new digitalformats ; a competing tape format was the inferior1/2 " VHS orbeta

name: Umbrella
desc: An Umbrella Made From Reflective Fabric Used For Bounce Lighting.

name: UMID (Unique Material Identifier)
desc: A SMPTE standard (SMPTE 300M/RP205) for metadata. The basic UMID contains 32 bytes of unique identification information (12 bytes identifying it as UMID data, followed by length and identification values). The extended UMID has an additional 32 bytes of information that contain “signature information” (time and data of creation, longitude, latitude, and altitude, as well as country, organization, and user codes).

name: UMTS (Universal Mobile Telecommunication System)
desc: A 3G mobile wireless telecommunications system whose standards are being developed by the Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP).

name: Unbalanced
desc: An Electrical Circuit In Which The Two Legs Of The Circuit Do Not Have The Identical Impedance To Ground. Often One Leg Is Also At Ground Poten-Tial. Unbalanced Circuit Connections Require Only Two Conductors (Signal “Hot” And Ground). Unbalanced Audio Circuitry Is Less Expensive To Build, But Under Certain Circumstances Is More Susceptible To Noise Pickup.

name: Unbalanced audio
desc: An audio signal consisting of one hot signal plus a shield/ground. More susceptible to noise than balanced audio over long cable runs.

name: Unbalanced Audio Signal
desc: Unbalanced systems use a signal and signal ground components. Shield conductors are sometimes employed as well. Interconnection of unbalanced signals is simple using relatively inexpensive cables and connectors such as the RCA phono jack.

name: Unbalanced Connection
desc: Consumer Audio Products Use Unbalanced Connections, Or Interfaces. An Unbalanced Connection Requires The Use Of Short Audio Cables And Is Susceptible To External Interference. An Unbalanced Interface Uses Coaxial Wire And Connectors With Two Electrical Contacts.

name: Unbalanced Line
desc: A line using two conductors to carry a signal, where one of the conductors is connected to ground.

name: Unbalanced Signal
desc: In CCTV, this refers to a type of video signal trans- mission through a coaxial cable. It is called unbalanced because the signal travels through the center core only, while the cable shield is used for equating the two voltage potentials between the coaxial cable ends.

name: unbilled role
desc: a ' supporting ' role for a major ( sometimes minor ) star that isofficially credited ( usually in the end credits ) , but no mention( or billing ) is made in the film's advertisements or the openingcredits ; contrast with cameo and uncredited role .

name: Uncompressed AV-over-IP
desc: AV distribution transporting video without lossy compression, preserving full signal integrity. Requires 10GbE or higher. Examples: SDVoE, SMPTE ST 2110.

name: Uncompressed-Quality Video
desc: Video that has the same image quality as uncompressed video, but has been compressed using mathematically lossless compression to optimize storage space.

name: Uncompressed Video
desc: A recorded or digitized video stream that is not processed by a data compression scheme. The video signal remains uncompressed at all stages of the process: input, storage, and output. Uncompressed video conforms to the ITU-R 601 standard.

name: uncredited role
desc: a role that a major ( or minor ) star plays that is not credited inthe credits or in the film's poster ; contrastwith cameo andunbilled role .

name: underacting
desc: refers to an understated , neutral and muted actingperformance ; contrast with overacting

name: Under-Cabinet  Lighting
desc: Luminaires Mounted On The Underside Of Cabinets To Provide Task Lighting, Typically In A Kitchen.

name: undercranking
desc: refers to the slowing down of a camera , by shooting at lessthan the standard 24 fps , so that the image , when normallyprojected , will appear in fast motion ; often used to produce acomic effect

name: underexposed
desc: refers to a film shot that has less light than normal , causing anindistinct , dimly - lit , unclear image ; the oppositeofoverexposed

name: Underexposed Shot
desc: The Picture Is Darker Because Of Insufficient Lighting. Shots From A Horror Film Are Good Examples, Such As The Lighting In A Haunted House Or A Midnight Chase Through The Cemetery.

name: Underfilled Launch Condition (ULC)
desc: In fiber optics, a condition where incoming light fills only a small percentage of the fiber core.

name: underground film
desc: a low - budget , non - commercial film , usually independentlymade , without the traditional sources of funding or distribution

name: Under Hung
desc: Hung From The Bottom Of A Beam Or Structure.

name: Underscan
desc: Most televisions use overscanning, resulting in some of the video being lost beyond the edges of the screen. Underscanning modifies the video timing so that the entire video signal appears in a rectangle centered on the television screen with a black border. The resolutions for square-pixel underscan and overscan images are:

name: Understudy
desc: An Actor Playing A Small Part In A Production Or, In The Professional Theatre, Often An Assistant Stage Manager, Who Has Learned And Rehearsed The Part Of One Of The Leading Actors To Take Over From Him/Her In The Event Of Illness Etc.

name: Underwriters' Laboratories, Inc. (Ul)
desc: An Independent, Not-For-Profit Organization Testing For Public Safety. This Organization Lists And Labels Products And Materials And Recognizes Parts, Components, And Materials, And Is Acceptable To Most Jurisdictional Authorities, E.G., Electrical Inspectors, Fire Marshals, Insurance Underwriters, And Governmental Agencies.

name: Undo/Redo
desc: The process that allows a return to the state of the edit immediately preceding the last edit or a repeat of an “undo” edit.

name: Unfold (Titan)
desc: An Avolites Titan function that lays out all steps of a chase onto the individual playback faders simultaneously, making it easy to edit individual chase steps without navigating menus.

name: Unicast
desc: Sending each user their own copy of a video (or other data) stream. As opposed to multicast, where one copy is sent and whoever wants it listens to that copy. It is the most commonly used method for video conferencing and video on demand today. Multicast, which is much more efficient, is slowly gaining ground, but required Internet Service Providers to support it.

name: Unidirectional
desc: a) A pickup pattern which is more sensitive to sounds arriving from one direction than from any other. b) Wire or group of wires in which data flows in only one direction. Each device connected to a unidirectional bus is either a transmitter, or a receiver, but not both.

name: Unidirectional Mike
desc: A microphone which picks up signals primarily from one direction and discriminates against or rejects sounds arriving from other directions.

name: Unidirectional Prediction
desc: A form of compression in which the codec uses information only from frames that have already been decompressed. Compare Bidirectional Prediction.

name: UNI (Ente Nazionale Italiano di Unificazione)
desc: Italian standardization body.

name: Uniform B-Spline
desc: A curve that rarely passes through its control point. Usually very smooth and may be controlled locally without generating breakpoints (cusps).

name: Uniformity
desc: The extent to which the output remains free from variations in amplitude. Uniformity is usually specified in terms of the positive and negative deviations from the average output within a roll, and in terms of the deviations in the average outputs between one roll and another.Uniformity is normally quoted in percent or dB.

name: Uni-Key
desc: A dedicated ISO keyer on the Vista switcher for use of a digital effects unit or character generator.

name: unions
desc: organizations that represent professionals in the motionpicture industry ( e.g. , directors , actors , writers , etc. ) , and helpthose individuals negotiate contracts , receive recognition ,pursue rights , protect interests , etc .; aka guilds|

name: Unit set
desc: A single multi-purpose stage setting used for an entire production, reconfigured or relit to represent different locations.

name: Unity
desc: A Game Engine Capable Of Creating 3D  On 2D Games On A Variety Of Platforms, Including Personal Computers, Mobile  Devices, Virtual  Reality, And Augmented Reality. The Game Engine Is Complemented By The Unity Asset  Store. A Range Of Pricing Plans Is Available, Including Free Versions For Personal Or Student Use.

name: Unity Gain
desc: A Circuit Or System That Has Its Voltage Gain Adjusted To Be One, Or Unity. A Signal Will Leave A Unity Gain Circuit At The Same Level At Which It Entered – No Amplification, But No Loss Either. In Mackie Mixers, Unity Gain Is Achieved By Set_x0002_Ting All Variable Controls To The Marked And Usu_x0002_Ally Detented “U” Setting. Mackie Mixers Are Optimized For Best Headroom And Noise Figures With All Gain Stages Beyond The Preamp Set At Unity Gain.

name: UNI (User-to-Network Interface)
desc: The interface between user equipment and private or public network equipment (e.g. ATM switches).

name: Universal DVD
desc: A DVD designed to play in DVD-Audio and DVD-Video players (by carrying a Dolby Digital audio track in the DVD-Video zone).

name: Universal DVD Player
desc: A DVD player that can play both DVD-Video and DVD-Audio discs.

name: Universal Label Code
desc: A code in the Universal Label created by con- catenating the first two sub-identifiers for ISO and ORG. For the SMPTE UL, this field must be “2B” in hexadecimal (hex) notation (0 x 2B).

name: Universal Label Data Key
desc: The 16-byte Universal Label that identifies the data being represented. Equivalent to “descriptor” in the terminology of MPEG-7 requirements.

name: Universal Label Header
desc: The first three octets of a Universal Label containing information unique to the label.

name: Universal Label (UL)
desc: A mechanism defined in SMPTE 298M used to identify the type and encoding of data within a general purpose data stream or file.

name: Universal Resource Locator (URL)
desc: A unique identification of the location of an elementary stream or an object descriptor.

name: Universe
desc: The Collection Of All Entities And The Space They Are Embedded In For A Vr World.

name: Unmanaged Switch
desc: A network switch requiring no user configuration. Configuration is locked to manufacturer specifications for simple plug-and-play use.

name: Unmodulated
desc: When used to describe television test signals, this term refers to pulses and pedestals which do not have high-frequency chromi- nance information added to them.

name: Unmount
desc: To make a file system that is accessible from a specific directory on a wo.rkstation temporarily inaccessible.UNO-CDR (Universal Networked Object-Common Data Representative).UNO-RPC (Universal Networked Object-Remote Procedure Call)

name: Unreal Engine
desc: A Game Engine Developed By Epic Games. Designed Primarily For 3D  Game Design, Unreal Engine Supports Personal Computers, Mobile  Devices, Virtual  Reality, And Augmented  Reality. Creators Can Buy And Sell Assets  On The Unreal Engine Marketplace. There Are Multiple License Options Depending On The Intended Use, Some Of Which Are Free-To-Use And/Or Royalty-Free.

name: unreliable narrator
desc: A literary term meaning a protagonist or narrator whoseperspective is skewed to their own perspective , producing aportrayal of events that may or may not be accurate ortruthful ; the lack of credibility may be deliberate or due to alack of knowledge

name: unspool
desc: a slang term meaning to screen or show a film

name: Unsqueezed Print
desc: A print in which the distorted image of an anamor- phic negative has not been corrected for normal projection.

name: Up converting
desc: The process of increasing pixels and/or frame rate and/or scanning format by interpolating existing pixels. Done from standard definition to high definition. Does not increase actual resolution.

name: Up converting (up-resing)
desc: The Process Which Increases The Number Of Pixels And/Or Frame Rate And/Or Scanning Format Used To Represent An Image By Interpolating Existing Pixels To Create New Ones At Closer Spacing. Despite Its Name The Process Does Not Increase The Resolution Of The Image. Up Converting Is Done From Standard Definition To High Definition.See Also: Down Converting, Side Converting.

name: Up Cut
desc: In editing, to cut the end of the previous scene, often by mistake. In general, to cut short.

name: Update
desc: An Eos command that records the current state of selected channels back into the active cue, palette, or preset without having to re-record. Useful for making incremental adjustments to existing programming.

name: Up-Down Buttons
desc: The replacement for potentiometers on AVC switch- ers. These allow three speeds of adjustment and may be assigned to any module. They offer a more compact package, and eliminate the problem of recalling an event that has different settings than the physical pots may have.

name: Upgrade
desc: Hardware that you add to the basic hardware that increases performance, such as additional memory (SIMMs) or faster graphics boards.

name: Uplight
desc: A Luminaire That Directs The Light Upward Onto The Ceiling And Upper Walls Of A Room.

name: Uplink
desc: The carrier used by Earth stations to transmit information to a satellite.

name: Upright
desc: Resting On Top Of A Beam Or Structure.

name: UPS
desc: Uninterruptible Power Supply. Provides immediate battery backup power during mains failure. Critical for AV control systems, servers, and signal processing equipment requiring continuous operation.

name: Upscaling
desc: The process of creating extra data from an incoming video stream to increase the image size by interpolating or replicating data before placing it into memory.

name: Up Stage
desc: The Portion Of The Stage That Is Furthest From The Audience. See “Raked Stage.”

name: Upstaging
desc: When one actor draws audience attention away from another, either by moving upstage (forcing the other to turn away) or through distracting behavior.

name: Upstream
desc: A term describing the precedence of an effect or key. The “stream” of video through a switcher allows multiple layers of effects to be accomplished, with each successive layer appearing on top of the previ- ous one. A module or effect whose video priority is lower, or underneath subsequent modules or effects is said to be upstream.

name: UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply)
desc: These are power supplies used in the majority of high security systems, whose purpose is to back-up the system for at least 10 minutes without mains power. The duration of this depends on the size of the UPS, usually expressed in VA, and the current consumption of the system itself.

name: Upward Light Ratio
desc: Some Lighting Schemes Will Require The Deliberate And Careful Use Of Upward Light – E.G. Ground Recessed Luminaires, Ground Mounted Floodlights, Festive Lighting – To Which These Limits Cannot Apply. However, Care Should Always Be Taken To Minimise Any Upward Waste Light By The Proper Application Of Suitably Directional Luminaires And Light Controlling Attachments.

name: URL
desc: Uniform Resource Locator. The address allowing a resource on the Internet to be identified, located, and accessed.

name: USB
desc: Universal Serial Bus. Standard for digital data and power over short distances. USB-A/B for peripherals; USB-C supports data, video (DisplayPort Alt Mode), and power (up to 240W). USB 3.2 reaches 20 Gbps; USB4 reaches 40 Gbps.

name: USB 1.1
desc: Universal Serial Bus 1.1. External bus standard supporting 12 Mbps and 1.5 Mbps data transfer. Supports up to 127 peripheral devices.

name: USB 2.0
desc: Universal Serial Bus 2.0. Supports up to 480 Mbps. Backward compatible with USB 1.1 using the same cables and connectors.

name: USB 3.2 Gen 1
desc: External bus standard supporting up to 5 Gbps. Also known as USB 3.0, SuperSpeed USB, or USB 3.1 Gen 1.

name: USB 3.2 Gen 2
desc: Released July 2013. Doubles USB 3.0 speed to 10 Gbps. Also known as USB 3.1 Gen 2 or SuperSpeed USB 10 Gbps.

name: USB 3.2 Gen 2x2
desc: Released September 2017. Two lanes of 10 Gbps = 20 Gbps. Requires certified USB Type-C cable. Also known as SuperSpeed USB 20 Gbps.

name: USB 4
desc: Released August 2019. Two lanes of 20 Gbps = 40 Gbps throughput. Backward compatible with USB 3.2 and USB 2.0.

name: USB-C
desc: Reversible USB connector supporting multiple protocols: USB 3.2 (20 Gbps), USB4 (40 Gbps), Thunderbolt 3/4 (40 Gbps), DisplayPort Alt Mode (video), and USB Power Delivery (up to 240W). Increasingly dominant in AV source devices.

name: USB-IF
desc: USB Implementers Forum. Promotes and supports USB specifications and compliance programs.

name: USB PD
